title: Reducing motion sickness for viewers of underwater footage
abstract: Methods, systems, and apparatus, including computer programs encoded on a computer storage medium, that receives input image data and analyzes the input image data by one or more computing devices. The input image data includes one or more images, and the one or more computing devices analyzes the input image data that includes one or more images and analyzes the input image data using one or more computing devices to determine that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness. In response to determining that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness, the one or more computing devices identify one or more actions to modify presentation of the input image data on a display device.
claims:
Claims 1. A method performed by one or more computers, the method comprising: receiving input image data comprising one or more images; analyzing, by one or more computing devices, the input image data to determine that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness; and in response to determining that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness, identifying, by the one or more computing devices, one or more actions to modify presentation of the input image data on a display device. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein analyzing the input image data to determine that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness comprises: providing the input image data to a machine-learning model trained using a corpus of videos that include a first set of videos identified as likely to induce motion-sickness, and a second set of videos identified as unlikely to induce motion sickness; and determining, based on an output obtained from the machine-learning model, that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein analyzing the input image data to determine that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness comprises: identifying a reference object in the input image data; determining that a relative motion of the reference object among multiple frames of the input image data satisfies a threshold condition; and in response to determining that the relative motion of the reference object among multiple frames of the input image data satisfies the threshold condition, determining that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness. 4. The method of claim 1, wherein analyzing the input image data to determine that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness comprises: receiving a user-profile associated with a viewer of the input image data; and determining that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness for the viewer based on user-behavior or user-preference information pertaining to motion sickness available within the user-profile. 5. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more actions comprises transmitting a command to adjust or stabilize a camera device capturing the input image data. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more actions comprise adjusting a frame rate associated with the presentation of the input image data on the display device from a first frame rate to a second frame rate that is less than the first frame rate. 7. The method of claim 1, wherein the one or more actions comprises downsampling the input image data to a lower resolution compared to an original resolution of the one or more images. 8. The method of claim 1, wherein applying the one or more actions comprises: generating one or more graphical or textual interfaces to represent substantive content of the input image data; and substituting a video presentation of the input image data on the display device with the one or more graphical or textual interfaces. 9. The method of claim 1, wherein applying the one or more actions comprises determining an audio signal to accompany the presentation of the input image data on the display device, wherein the audio signal is selected in accordance with relative movement of one or more objects among frames of the input image data. 10. The method of claim 1, wherein modifying the presentation of the input image data on the display device comprises substituting a first number of views associated with the input image data with a second number of views associated with the input image data, in which the second number of views is smaller than the first number of views. 11. The method of claim 1 , wherein modifying the presentation of the input image data on the display device comprises adjusting a first aspect ratio for a first view associated with the input image data to a second aspect ratio, wherein the second aspect ratio is greater than the first aspect ratio. 12. The method of claim 1, wherein modifying the presentation of the input image data on the display device comprises shifting the presentation of the input image data from a first 

position to a second position, wherein the second position is closer to a center of the display device than the first position. 13. The method of claim 1, wherein modifying the presentation of the input image data on the display device comprises substituting a view associated with the input image data with a graph associated with the input image data. 14. The method of claim 1 , wherein modifying the presentation of the input image data on the display device comprises hiding the presentation of the input image data for a duration of time. 15. A non -transitory computer-readable medium storing one or more instructions executable by a computer system to perform operations comprising: receiving input image data comprising one or more images; analyzing, by one or more computing devices, the input image data to determine that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness; and in response to determining that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness, identify ing, by the one or more computing devices, one or more actions to modify presentation of the input image data on a display device. 16. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 15, wherein analyzing the input image data to determine that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness comprises : providing the input image data to a machine-learning model trained using a corpus of videos that include a first set of videos identified as likely to induce motion-sickness, and a second set of videos identified as unlikely to induce motion sickness; and determining, based on an output obtained from the machine-learning model, that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness. 17. The non-transitory computer-readable medium of claim 15, wherein analyzing the input image data to determine that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness comprises : identify ing a reference object in the input image data; determining that a relative motion of the reference object among multiple frames of the input image data satisfies a threshold condition; and 

 in response to determining that the relative motion of the reference object among multiple frames of the input image data satisfies the threshold condition, determining that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness. 18. A system comprising: one or more computers; and one or more computer-readable media storing instructions that are operable, when executed by the one or more computers, to perform operations comprising: receiving input image data comprising one or more images; analyzing, by one or more computing devices, the input image data to determine that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness; and in response to determining that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness, identifying, by the one or more computing devices, one or more actions to modify presentation of the input image data on a display device. 19. The system of claim 18. wherein analyzing the input image data to determine that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness comprises: providing the input image data to a machine-learning model trained using a corpus of videos that include a first set of videos identified as likely to induce motion-sickness, and a second set of videos identified as unlikely to induce motion sickness; and determining, based on an output obtained from the machine-learning model, that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness. 20. The system of claim 18, wherein analyzing the input image data to determine that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness comprises: identifying a reference object in the input image data; determining that a relative motion of the reference object among multiple frames of the input image data satisfies a threshold condition; and in response to determining that the relative motion of the reference object among multiple frames of the input image data satisfies the threshold condition, determining that the input image data is likely to induce motion sickness.
